title: septilia caroliniana
side_effects: Septilia caroliniana can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, allergic reactions, or interactions with certain medications. Some people may experience nausea, vomiting, or stomach cramps after ingesting the plant's leaves or roots. In severe cases, improper use of septilia caroliniana can lead to symptoms like rapid heartbeat, difficulty breathing, or even seizures. It's essential to consult with a medical professional or a qualified herbalist before using this plant for medicinal purposes.
